checklist.question: Uses `defuddle parse <url>` CLI command — not WebFetch, curl, or browser automation?
Always includes `--md` flag for markdown output?
Checks if defuddle is installed and runs `npm install -g defuddle` if not?
Uses `-o <file>` flag when saving output to a file?
Uses `-p <name>` flag for extracting specific metadata (title, description, domain)?
Preferred over WebFetch for standard web pages (articles, docs, blog posts)?
URL is passed as a direct argument — not piped or fetched separately?
Does not use `--json` unless user specifically requests JSON format?
Presents extracted content cleanly to user — not raw CLI debug output?
Falls back to WebFetch only for dynamic/JS-heavy pages where defuddle fails?

stats.last_improvement: 2026-03-27: Added explicit fallback decision tree (defuddle -> WebFetch -> agent-browser) and multi-format guidance. Baseline 0.94 -> 0.98.
